title: Food delivery and takeaway
description: Tracking the order every 30 seconds, tipping the driver in the rain and that disappointment when the chips are cold — takeaway culture is universal! See if your habits match!

questions.text: Name a reason people order food delivery instead of cooking.

answers.text: too tired or lazy to cook
it's a treat or reward day
having people over and no time

questions.text: Name a cuisine people order most for delivery.
Name something people do while waiting for a delivery.

answers.text: track the order live every few minutes
get hungrier the longer it takes

questions.text: What do people say when the food arrives cold?

answers.text: contact the app for a refund
nothing — eat it anyway because hungry

questions.text: Name something missing from the order that people notice too late.

answers.text: the sauces or dips they paid for

questions.text: Name something people always regret ordering for delivery.

answers.text: fish and chips — soggy by arrival
fancy food that looks nothing like the photo

questions.text: What do people add to the order at the last minute?
Name something people feel guilty about after a big takeaway.

answers.text: not cooking the healthy meal they planned

questions.text: When do people most often order a takeaway?

answers.text: after a long hard day at work
when watching a big game or event
when there's literally nothing at home

questions.text: Name something people say when someone questions the cost of ordering in.

answers.text: I work hard and deserve it
it saves time which is valuable
once in a while it's fine
cooking also costs money and time
